facts.text: The Hammocks takes its name from the Spanish word hamaca, meaning hammock or swing, a nod to the area's natural landscape.
Developed in the mid‑1970s, The Hammocks turned roughly 1,100 acres of tropical hardwood hammock forests into a master‑planned suburban community.
The Hammocks sits about 6 miles west of Kendall and 18 miles southwest of downtown Miami, placing it firmly within the Miami metro area.
Public schools in The Hammocks are part of the Miami‑Dade County Public Schools system, serving the community’s families.
Alienware’s headquarters are located in The Hammocks, giving the suburb a connection to a major tech brand.
A greenway system links The Hammocks’ parks, schools, library, police and fire stations to a central town center, weaving the neighborhoods together.
